description: Replace flaky fixed sleeps in permissions.e2e.ts with deterministic waits. The test file uses `await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 1_000))` in two places to wait for page load after browser_open_tab, while every other E2E test file uses deterministic waits (waitForFunction, openTestAppTab, or polling helpers). On slow CI these 1s sleeps may be insufficient; on fast machines they waste time.

userStories.title: Replace fixed 1s sleeps in permissions.e2e.ts with deterministic page load waits
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the permissions E2E tests to use deterministic waits instead of fixed sleeps so that the tests are reliable on slow CI and don't waste time on fast machines.
acceptanceCriteria: The `await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 1_000))` on line 142 of e2e/permissions.e2e.ts (in the 'interact-tier tool on non-trusted domain' test) is replaced with a deterministic wait that verifies the page has loaded

The `await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 1_000))` on line 357 of e2e/permissions.e2e.ts (in the 'trusted domain auto-allow' test) is replaced with a deterministic wait that verifies the page has loaded
No fixed-duration `setTimeout` sleeps remain in e2e/permissions.e2e.ts for page load waiting
Both affected tests still pass (the confirmation timeout test and trusted domain test)
userStories.notes:
File: e2e/permissions.e2e.ts

There are two instances of fixed sleeps used to wait for page load after browser_open_tab:

1. Line 142 (in 'interact-tier tool on non-trusted domain triggers confirmation progress notification' test):
   ```
   await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 1_000));
   ```
   This waits after opening a tab with browser_open_tab before calling browser_navigate_tab.

2. Line 357 (in 'interact-tier tool on trusted domain (localhost) succeeds without confirmation' test):
   ```
   await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 1_000));
   ```
   This waits after opening a tab with browser_open_tab before calling browser_navigate_tab.

The correct pattern used throughout other tests: after calling browser_open_tab, use the returned tabId to navigate. The browser_open_tab tool already waits for the tab to be created. The navigate tool itself handles navigation. So the sleep may be unnecessary entirely — the navigate_tab tool handles loading the new URL.

Alternative approach: Use browser_navigate_tab directly (which handles its own loading) or add a short poll using `callToolExpectSuccess` to verify the tab is in a usable state. The simplest fix is to check if removing the sleep entirely works (browser_navigate_tab handles its own navigation wait). If that's not stable, use `mcpClient.callTool('browser_execute_script', { tabId, code: 'document.readyState' })` to poll until loaded.

IMPORTANT: This test file defines its own custom Playwright fixtures (PermissionFixtures) that start the server with OPENTABS_SKIP_CONFIRMATION: '' — do not change this. The permissions tests need the confirmation system active.
